summary: Applied the short-lived credential and internal mTLS policy live by verifying step-ca-issued SSH certificates for routine operator access and enforcing step-ca-backed client-certificate authentication on the private OpenBao API.

verification.observed: A controller-issued eight-hour ops SSH certificate was minted through https://100.118.189.95:9443 and used successfully against the Proxmox host and docker-runtime-lv3 through the managed CA trust path.
A controller-issued one-hour client certificate successfully queried https://100.118.189.95:8200/v1/sys/health and received initialized=true, sealed=false, standby=false.
The same health request without a client certificate failed during TLS negotiation with curl exit code 56 and no HTTP response.
make converge-openbao refreshed controller-automation and mail-platform AppRole artifacts with one-use secret IDs and a 15-minute secret_id_ttl after managed verification.

notes: OpenBao keeps a loopback HTTP listener on 127.0.0.1:8201 for managed bootstrap and verification while the externally published operator path on :8200 now requires mTLS.

The controller-side SSH verification uses a temporary unencrypted key written with step ssh certificate --insecure --no-password so non-interactive SSH can sign with the freshly issued short-lived certificate.
